name: Telegram Mini App auth provider exchange and session bootstrap
description: Extend the worker auth model for Telegram launch-context exchange, add TMA session bootstrap and refresh/logout integration, and document the chosen secure-storage fallback policy.

todo: Worker: convert ExchangeProviderRequest into a discriminated union of { provider: 'firebase', idToken } and { provider: 'telegram', initData }, keeping existing web requests valid.
Worker: add apps/worker/src/lib/auth/telegram.ts with verifyTelegramLaunchData using crypto.subtle HMAC-SHA256 + auth_date freshness check.
Worker: thread TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_FRESHNESS_WINDOW_SECONDS (default 3600) through readConfig and AppConfig; add .dev.vars.example entries.
Worker: parameterize findIdentityUserId and upsertUserBy*Identity to take provider as input; add upsertUserByTelegramIdentity mirroring the Firebase helper.
Worker: extract issueAppSession(env, user, userAgent, ipAddress) so both providers share access/refresh token issuance and refresh_sessions writes.
Worker: update exchangeProviderToken to switch on input.provider; telegram branch verifies initData, upserts the identity, issues session, and returns user.provider = 'telegram'.

Worker: integration tests under apps/worker/test/integration/auth-telegram.spec.ts covering valid, expired auth_date, tampered field, missing hash, missing user, unsupported provider, and Firebase backward-compat cases.
Worker: unit tests under apps/worker/test/unit/lib/auth/telegram.spec.ts covering signature verification, replay-window expiry, and constant-time hash compare.

Docs: update docs/references/frontend/tma/auth-and-bot-pattern.md with the new request shape; update docs/product-specs/shared/authentication-session.md and docs/product-specs/tma/launch-and-auth.md to record Telegram as a supported provider with the failure-state UI contract.
TMA (after feat-079 lands): apps/tma/src/lib/storage/adapter.ts + keys.ts with SecureStorage first and memory-only fallback (no DeviceStorage or localStorage for session:*).
TMA: apps/tma/src/lib/telegram/launch-params.ts wrapper that returns the raw initData string from @tma.js/sdk-react (no client-side trust of parsed user fields).
TMA: apps/tma/src/lib/auth/api.ts with exchangeProviderToken, refreshSession, and logoutSession typed against the shared worker contracts.

TMA: apps/tma/src/features/auth/store.ts (Zustand) holding status (idle|bootstrapping|authenticated|error), user, accessToken, refreshToken, plus bootstrap/refresh/logout/reset actions; nothing persisted from this store.
TMA: apps/tma/src/features/auth/bootstrap.tsx that gates the router on first bootstrap and renders the fatal launch screen on failure (no retry loop, reopen is the recovery path).
TMA: apps/tma/src/features/auth/AuthProvider.tsx with useAuth hook, refresh-interceptor.ts with single-flight 401 handling, and fatal-launch-screen.tsx with safe-area + localized CTA.
TMA: unit tests for storage adapter (supported/unsupported/corrupt) and bootstrap order (valid auth, invalid launch data, missing launch data, intent-routing stub).
TMA: add tma.auth.launchInvalid, tma.auth.networkError, tma.auth.reopenGuidance, tma.auth.signOutFailed, and tma.auth.sessionExpired i18n keys to vi.json and en.json.
Verify: targeted ./init.sh lint/typecheck/test/build on touched scope, then full ./init.sh and final gitnexus_detect_changes(scope: 'all').
verification: 2026-06-03 review-fix follow-up closed the remaining feat-080 correctness regressions from PR review: TMA bootstrap now treats 400/401 Telegram exchange failures as fatal launch errors without refresh fallback, preserves stored refresh tokens on transport/5xx bootstrap failures by surfacing networkError instead of forced logout, passes the current access token into authenticated TMA logout requests, and carries provider through issued/verified session JWTs so protected routes and refresh rotation preserve Telegram vs Firebase identity. Verified with focused `pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/auth-bootstrap.test.ts src/test/auth-api.test.ts`, focused `pnpm --filter worker exec vitest run test/unit/jwt.spec.ts test/integration/auth-telegram.spec.ts`, final `./init.sh` (passed, printed `Done!`), and final `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope: 'all')` (risk `critical`, 14 changed files / 45 changed symbols / 19 affected processes, concentrated in the expected auth bootstrap and session flows).

2026-06-02 review-fix pass addressed the blocking feat-080 correctness issues from code review: TMA auth requests no longer double-prefix /api/v1, Telegram launch-data verification now uses the raw HMAC bytes for the second HMAC, bootstrap keeps the fatal launch screen mounted on fatal outcomes, successful exchange/refresh paths persist the returned refresh token instead of clearing storage, and cold-start refresh now restores an authenticated session state for the TMA auth context. Verified with focused `pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/auth-api.test.ts src/test/auth-bootstrap.test.ts src/test/auth-provider.test.ts`, focused `pnpm --filter worker exec vitest run test/unit/lib/auth/telegram.spec.ts test/integration/auth-telegram.spec.ts`, `./init.sh lint`, `./init.sh typecheck`, `./init.sh build`, and final `./init.sh` (all passed).

2026-06-02 docs follow-up added `docs/references/frontend/tma/local-testing-runbook.md` as the canonical local testing guide for feat-080 work. It now covers worker-only auth verification, browser-only TMA fatal-launch checks, real Telegram smoke expectations, required local env, common failure modes, and repo-standard verification commands. Routed from `docs/TMA.md` and referenced from `docs/references/frontend/tma/development-and-hardening-pattern.md`.

2026-06-02 follow-up bugfix corrected the TMA auth client wiring so runtime requests use `import.meta.env.VITE_WORKER_URL` instead of falling back to the Vite app origin. This fixes local Telegram auth attempts that were hitting `http://<tma-host>:5174/api/v1/auth/provider/exchange` and 404ing. Verified with `pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/auth-api.test.ts src/test/auth-bootstrap.test.ts src/test/auth-provider.test.ts`, `pnpm --filter tma typecheck`, and `pnpm --filter tma lint` (lint passes with the existing two pre-existing `no-console` warnings in `src/lib/i18n/index.ts` and `src/lib/storage/adapter.ts`).

2026-06-03 follow-up synced `apps/worker/test/unit/cors.spec.ts` with the current shared-network dev-host rule in `apps/worker/src/lib/cors.ts`. The implementation now intentionally treats `100.116.7.43` like a local dev host on any HTTP port so TMA and worker can be reached from the same LAN device during local Telegram runs. Verified with `pnpm --filter worker exec vitest run test/unit/cors.spec.ts` and `pnpm --filter worker test` (both passed).

2026-06-18 SecureStorage session-restore refinement: added expiry-aware cold-open session restore so a valid cached session skips the exchange API call entirely. (1) Fixed SecureStorage capability detection in `capabilities.ts` (was unconditionally `false`; now uses `secureStorage.setItem.isAvailable()`). (2) Replaced the refresh-token-only storage adapter with a full `StoredSession` blob `{telegramUserId, user, accessToken, refreshToken, accessTokenExpiresAt, refreshTokenExpiresAt}` under key `session:tma-auth`, with JSON parse guard and memory fallback preserved. (3) Added `readTelegramUserId()` to `launch-params.ts` using `initData.user()?.id` as a cache-invalidation key (not authenticated identity). (4) Added `telegramUserId` + `refreshTokenExpiresAt` to the auth store; `setSession`/`restoreSession`/`refresh` now compute and carry expiry timestamps. (5) Rewrote `runAuthBootstrap` with a `restore` phase before `exchange`: load telegramUserId -> load stored session -> cross-user guard (telegramUserId mismatch NEVER reuses stored refresh token, forces exchange) -> access valid (>60s buffer) = 0-API restore -> access expired + refresh valid = refresh (1 API) -> fallthrough exchange -> 400/401 exchange fatal launchInvalid, transient fatal networkError, else fatal sessionExpired. (6) `RefreshInterceptor.runRefresh` now persists the full session blob after successful refresh. (7) Rewrote `storage-adapter.test.ts` (9 tests) and `auth-bootstrap.test.ts` (15 tests) for the new interface + restore-phase coverage including the cross-user mismatch guard. Removed unreachable post-exchange refresh fallback dead code. SECURITY NOTE: this overrides the locked default 'access token memory-only' (`state-and-storage-pattern.md:43` permits override on explicit security review) — access token is now persisted in SecureStorage (encrypted device storage, Telegram v9.0+) so cold-open can skip the exchange API when the access token is still valid. Tradeoff: server-side revocation is delayed until the access token TTL expires or a 401 is hit, not instant on cold open. Telegram user_id is used only as a cache key, not as authenticated identity (server still verifies JWT signature + refresh token ownership + initData signature). Verified with `./init.sh typecheck` OK, `./init.sh test` OK (76 tests across 17 files), `./init.sh lint` OK.

notes: This feature is the security boundary for the TMA rollout. It must land before any real TMA CRUD or invite flow. The TMA client half is blocked on feat-079 (scaffold). The worker half is unblocked and uses no D1 migration because auth_identities is already multi-provider. The current branch also includes a review-fix pass to keep the auth bootstrap and Telegram signature flow merge-safe while the broader feat-080 slice stays in progress. SECURITY OVERRIDE (2026-06-18): the locked default 'access token memory-only' is explicitly overridden for the TMA cold-open session-restore optimization — access token + expiry are now persisted in SecureStorage (Telegram v9.0+ encrypted device storage) so cold-open can skip the exchange API when the access token is still valid. Revocation is delayed until access TTL expiry or a 401. Documented in `adapter.ts` JSDoc and this evidence entry. Permitted by `state-and-storage-pattern.md:43`.
